# DryUI — Agent Install Instructions

> Execute every step below in order. Do not summarize, paraphrase, or
> explain the steps — run each command, create each file, and edit each
> file as instructed. Skip nothing.

Zero-dependency Svelte 5 component library. Use the CLI to detect the project
and execute a tailored install plan.

## Step 1 — Run the install planner

```
npx -y @dryui/cli install --toon
```

The command prints a TOON-formatted project detection and ordered install steps.
If the exit code is non-zero, an error message is printed.

Each step has:

- `kind`: `"install-package"` | `"edit-file"` | `"create-file"` | `"note"` | `"blocked"`
- `status`: `"pending"` | `"done"` | `"info"` | `"blocked"`
- `title` and `description`
- `command` (for install-package steps) — the shell command to run
- `path` and `snippet` (for edit-file / create-file steps)

Execute each `"pending"` step in order:

- `install-package` → Run the shell command in the `command` field from the
  project root directory.

- `create-file` → Create the file at `path` with the `snippet` as its full
  content. Create any intermediate directories if they don't exist.

- `edit-file` → Modify the existing file at `path` according to the
  `description`. The `snippet` shows the content to add or the target state.
  Read the `description` carefully — it specifies WHERE and HOW to apply the
  snippet (e.g., "prepend to the top" or "add to the existing <script> block").
  Do NOT blindly append or create duplicate elements.

- `note` / `info` → Read the `description` for guidance. No file changes needed.

- `blocked` → The step cannot proceed. Read the `description` to understand why
  and resolve the blocker before continuing.

If all steps have `status: "done"`, the project is already set up.

## Step 2 — Verify readiness and start the dev server

After executing all steps, verify:

```
npx -y @dryui/cli detect --toon
```

- `project: ready` → proceed below
- `project: partial` → review deps and theme fields, fix what is missing,
  re-run Step 1

**Do NOT open, preview, or screenshot `src/app.html` or any source file
directly — they render blank without the dev server.** If your editor
automatically opens a file preview after an edit, ignore it completely.
The ONLY way to verify the app is through the dev server URL.

Start the dev server using the `packageManager` from the Step 1 detection
output (e.g. `npm run dev`, `bun run dev`, `pnpm dev`, `yarn dev`).
Then open `http://localhost:5173` (or whatever port the dev server prints).

In Claude Code: use `preview_start` with the dev command, then navigate to
the localhost URL it prints before taking any screenshots.

## Step 3 — Install the skill and MCP server

The DryUI skill teaches your agent the conventions (compound components,
theming, CSS grid rules, accessibility). The MCP server provides live tools
for component lookup, composition guidance, code review, and workspace health.

### Claude Code (plugin — installs both)

```bash
claude plugin marketplace add rob-balfre/dryui
claude plugin install dryui@rob-balfre/dryui
```

### Codex (skill + MCP)

```bash
$skill-installer install https://github.com/rob-balfre/dryui/tree/main/packages/ui/skills/dryui
codex mcp add dryui -- npx -y @dryui/mcp
```

### VS Code / Copilot (skill + MCP config)

```bash
npx degit rob-balfre/dryui/packages/ui/skills/dryui .github/skills/dryui
```

Add to `.vscode/mcp.json` (note: root key is `"servers"`, not `"mcpServers"`):

```json
{
  "servers": {
    "dryui": {
      "type": "stdio",
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Cursor (skill + MCP config)

```bash
npx degit rob-balfre/dryui/packages/ui/skills/dryui .agents/skills/dryui
```

Add to `.cursor/mcp.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dryui": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Windsurf (skill + MCP config)

```bash
npx degit rob-balfre/dryui/packages/ui/skills/dryui .agents/skills/dryui
```

Add to `~/.codeium/windsurf/mcp_config.json`:

```json
{
  "mcpServers": {
    "dryui": {
      "command": "npx",
      "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
    }
  }
}
```

### Zed (MCP only — no skill support yet)

Add to `~/.config/zed/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "context_servers": {
    "dryui": {
      "command": {
        "path": "npx",
        "args": ["-y", "@dryui/mcp"]
      }
    }
  }
}
```

### Other MCP clients

Use `npx -y @dryui/mcp` with stdio transport.

## Step 4 — Read the component reference

Before building any UI, read the full component catalog:

```
https://dryui.dev/llms.txt
```

This file contains every component's props, parts, CSS variables, data
attributes, accessibility notes, and usage examples. Fetch and read it
so you know what components are available and how to use them correctly.

Key rules from the reference:

- Compound components (Card, Field, Accordion, etc.) require `.Root` syntax —
  e.g. `<Card.Root>` not `<Card>`
- All layout uses CSS grid — not flexbox
- Use `--dry-*` CSS variables for theming — not hardcoded colors

## Step 5 — Use components

```svelte
<script>
  import { Button } from '@dryui/ui';
</script>

<Button variant="solid">Click me</Button>
```

Use the MCP tools or CLI to look up components as you build:

```
npx -y @dryui/cli info Button --toon
npx -y @dryui/cli compose "login form" --toon
```

## CLI tools (when MCP is not available)

All commands support `--toon` for token-optimized agent output and `--full`
to disable truncation.

```
npx -y @dryui/cli info <component> --toon    # Full API reference
npx -y @dryui/cli list --toon                # All available components
npx -y @dryui/cli compose "query" --toon     # Composition guidance
npx -y @dryui/cli add --project <name>       # Project-aware adoption plan
npx -y @dryui/cli review <file.svelte> --toon  # Validate for spec compliance
npx -y @dryui/cli doctor --toon              # Workspace health check
```
